body.title: TOO pass: add why to every architecture point
body.result: TOO pass: why annotations added to UU CLAUDE.md rules, sprint-ticket/context-load hard rules, 5 palace rule nodes (coding/commits/database/budget/capability-protocol) via UPDATE
body.description:
Every architecture decision, design rule, palace node, skill, and CLAUDE.md entry should carry its why — Theory of Operation (TOO) pattern from radar/avionics systems docs where every circuit node has both what and why. Without the why, future models and humans revert or 'simplify' decisions they don't understand.

The /sorted hypothesis extraction does some of this at decision time. This ticket is the retroactive pass: audit existing architecture points and annotate any that lack a why.

Priority targets:
- palace nodes under theigors/rules/* — design rules without rationale
- Skills (SKILL.md files) — steps without why comments
- CLAUDE.md entries — rules stated without justification
- lab/design_docs/decisions/* — decisions without hypothesis/measurement fields

Going forward: every new architecture point written by CC must include a why inline. The 5.Skills.md /sorted rename fix (2026-05-28) is the model — rule + why-for-the-rule.

**Affected files:** palace nodes (DB), skills/*.md, CLAUDE.md, lab/design_docs/decisions/
**Design rules:** none apply — this IS the design rule
**Scope boundary:** annotation pass only; no structural changes; flag missing whys, add them
**Test plan:** no automated test — review pass; done when top-20 highest-traffic palace rules have why fields
